title: dryopteris mascarensis
intro: Dryopteris mascarensis, also known as Mascarene wood fern, is a species of fern native to the Mascarene Islands and Réunion Island. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Dryopteris mascarensis are said to include reducing fever, soothing respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing, as well as possessing antiseptic and antimicrobial properties. Dryopteris mascarensis is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional remedies for skin conditions and digestive issues.
